sn11.11:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, in the past, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he adopted and undertook seven vows by the undertaking of which he achieved the status of Sakka.What were the seven vows?
sn11.11:1.2: (1) "‘As long as I live may I support my parents.’
sn11.11:1.3: (2) "‘As long as I live may I respect the family elders.’
sn11.11:1.4: (3) "‘As long as I live may I speak gently.’
sn11.11:1.5: (4) "‘As long as I live may I not speak divisively.’
sn11.11:1.6: (5) "‘As long as I live may I dwell at home with a mind devoid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, delighting in relinquishment, devoted to charity,delighting in giving and sharing.’
sn11.11:1.7: (6) "‘As long as I live may I speak the truth.’
sn11.11:1.8: (7) "‘As long as I live may I be free from anger, and if anger should arise in me may I dispel it quickly.’
sn11.11:1.9: "In the past, bhikkhus, when Sakka, lord of the devas, was a human being, he adopted and undertook these seven vows by the undertaking of which he achieved the status of Sakka.
